intent: Write a small-cap buying brief on 'vitamin and supplement basics' grounded by encyclopedia facts rather than reviews. (1) One Stop Market shopping site — browse the health-household category and collect at least 3 supplement products (multivitamin, vitamin D, omega-3, or similar). Record name, price, rating, review count. (2) Wikipedia — open 'Dietary supplement' and 'Vitamin D' (or most relevant vitamin article) and extract 3-4 facts on recommended daily allowance and evidence. The report must: (a) present the storefront picks with stats, (b) match each to the Wikipedia-documented recommendation, (c) flag any product whose marketing exceeds Wikipedia's evidence base, (d) close with one sentence on 'start here if you start anywhere'. Cite every claim.
author_notes: Easy tier. Shopping + Wikipedia only (no Reddit). Good for testing non-social fact check.
